destinations.evidence: The active-job predicate returns false for missing or expired jobs and true only for nonzero executor with future expiry.

equivalence_argument: Recommitment checks can use job_exists_and_active so lingering expired storage does not count as an active job; only future-expiring jobs block duplicates.
notes: The helper is expiry-aware and treats expired jobs as non-existent for commitment-duplication purposes.
rationale: The new job_exists_and_active helper reads both jobExecutor and expiryBlock, returning false for expired jobs so lazy cleanup does not permanently block recommitment. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When checking whether an async commitment's job already exists, the system must treat a job as active only if jobExecutor[id] is non-zero and expiryBlock[id] is greater than the block being built or verified; expired jobs with lingering storage must not block a fresh commitment for the same transaction hash.
suggested_falsification_tests: Mock AsyncJobTracker storage for missing job, active job, and expired job; assert job_exists_and_active returns false, true, and false respectively at the target block.
summary: Expired async jobs do not block recommitment.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Mock AsyncJobTracker storage for missing job, active job, and expired job; assert job_exists_and_active returns false, true, and false respectively at the target block.
title: Expired async jobs do not block recommitment
